top_signals.title: Give me home Euros over World Cup, but is it really worth £557m of taxpayers’ money?
top_signals.summary: I’m sure I’ll enjoy much of the 2026 World Cup, provided I can stay awake late enough, but I’ll approach Euro 2028 with far greater enthusiasm.  Home nations competing on home soil in British Summer Time and with advertisers and broadcasters whipping up interest among the general populace. What’s not to like, other than violent [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Legal Expert Lipton on SpaceX Shareholder Disempowerment
top_signals.summary: University of Colorado Boulder Professor of Law and Laurence W. DeMuth Chair of Business Law Ann M. Lipton highlights how the SpaceX initial public offering structure limits investors' rights. Because of super voting shares, she notes that public shareholders have no votes, no sales, no suits. She speaks with Haslinda Amin on "Insight with Haslinda Amin." (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: UK and European Stocks Set to Fall as Volatile Week Continues
Mining M&A value surges in Q1, but deal count plunges: S&P
top_signals.summary: At $26.28 billion, the total value of deals represents the second highest ever for a quarter since the firm began tracking the data.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
